externalDocs.description: Find out more about Netatmo API

get.description:
This endpoint permits to retrieve the actual topology and static information of all devices present into a user account.
It is possible to specify a home_id to focus on one home or to specify a gateway_type to focus on one type of gateway.


Scope to use:
read_thermostat

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Filter by Home ID

parameters.name: gateway_types
parameters.description: Filter by Gateway Types {NAPlug, OTH, BNS}

get.summary: Get the current status of a home and the associated devices
get.description:
This endpoint permits to retrieve the actual status of all devices present into a specific home.
It is possible to specify a list of gateways using device_types to filter the returned devices.

Scope to use:
read_thermostat

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: device_types
parameters.description: Filter by Gateway Types {NAPlug, OTH, BNS}

get.summary: Retrieve data history of a room (eg to display graphs)
get.description:
This endpoint permits to retrieve the history of data associated to a room.

Scope to use:
read_thermostat

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: room_id
parameters.description: Room ID

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Timeframe between two measurements {30min, 1hour, 3hours, 1day, 1week, 1month}

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Type of data to be returned {temperature, sp_temperature, min_temp, max_temp, date_min_temp, date_max_temp}
date_min_temp & date_max_temp are only available for large scales ({1day, 1week, 1month}).

parameters.name: date_begin
parameters.description:
Timestamp of the first measure to retrieve (Local Unix Time in seconds).
By default, it will retrieve the oldest data available.

parameters.name: date_end
parameters.description: Timestamp of the last measure to retrieve (Local Unix Time in seconds)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of measurements (default and max are 1024)

parameters.name: optimize
parameters.description:
Optimized format of the answer. Default is true.
For mobile apps we recommend True to save bandwidth. If bandwidth isn't an issue, we recommend False as it is easier to parse.

parameters.name: real_time
parameters.description:
To get real timestamp. Default is false.
If scale different than max, timestamps are by default offset + scale/2.
To get exact timestamps, use true.

post.summary: Control the heating of a room
post.description:
This endpoint permits to control the heating of a specific room. A room can be set in 3 differents modes:
  • "manual" mode in which setpoints can be applied for a specific duration (use endtime)
  • "max" mode which will put the setpoint at max (30°) for a given duration (default is xx and can be changed by the user in its settings)
  • "home" mode which basically makes the room following the Home mode.

Scope to use:
write_thermostat

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: room_id
parameters.description: Room ID

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: The mode you are applying to this room {manual, max, home}

parameters.name: temp
parameters.description: Manual temperature to apply. Required if mode is manual.

parameters.name: endtime
parameters.description: End time of the schedule mode set (Local Unix Time in seconds).

post.description:
This endpoint permits to control the heating of a specific home. A home can be set in 3 differents modes:
  • "schedule" mode in which the home will follow the user schedule
  • "away" mode which will put the whole house to away (default is 12° but can be changed by the user in its settings)
  • "hg" corresponds to frostguard mode (7° by default)

Scope to use:
write_thermostat

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Set the home heating system to use {schedule, away, hg}

parameters.name: endtime
parameters.description: Enddate of the therm mode (Local Unix Time in seconds). It's settable only for "hg" and "away" mode

get.description:
This endpoint permits to retrieve the history of the boiler data.

Scope to use:
read_thermostat

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Gateway Mac Address

parameters.name: module_id
parameters.description: Module Mac Adress

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Timeframe between two measurements {30min, 1hour, 3hours, 1day, 1week, 1month}

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Type of data to be returned {boileron, boileroff, sum_boiler_on, sum_boiler_off}
Boileron/off is only available for scales from 30 to 3hours and sums of boiler on/off for scales from 1day to 1month.
  • boileron = Average number of minutes the boiler is active per hour
  • boileroff = Average number of minutes the boiler is inactive per hour
  • sum_boiler_on = Sum of minutes the boiler is active during the scale time period
  • sum_boiler_off = Sum of minutes the boiler is inactive during the scale time period

parameters.name: date_begin
parameters.description:
Timestamp of the first measure to retrieve (Local Unix Time in seconds).
By default, it will retrieve the oldest data available.

parameters.name: date_end
parameters.description: Timestamp of the last measure to retrieve (Local Unix Time in seconds)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of measurements (default and max are 1024)

parameters.name: optimize
parameters.description:
Optimized format of the answer. Default is true.
For mobile apps we recommend True to save bandwidth. If bandwidth isn't an issue, we recommend False as it is easier to parse.

parameters.name: real_time
parameters.description: If scale different than max, timestamps are by default offset + scale/2. To get exact timestamps, use true. Default is false.

post.description:
This endpoint permits to create a new thermostat schedule of a home.
Important notes:
  • A schedule must contain all the rooms of a Home where a heating module is installed
  • Zones Confort (=0), Night (=1) & Eco (=4) are mandatory


Scope to use:
write_thermostat

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the schedule

parameters.name: hg_temp
parameters.description: Frost guard temperature value

parameters.name: away_temp
parameters.description: Away temperature value

parameters.name: zones
parameters.description: Array of zones. Zones describe the heating behavior in each room. More info on the format of a zone here.

parameters.name: timetable
parameters.description: Array of timetables. Timetables apply a zone for a specific duration. More info on the format of a timetable here.

post.description:
This endpoint permits to edit a thermostat schedule of a home.
Important notes:
  • A schedule must contain all the rooms of a Home
  • Zones Confort (=0), Night (=1) & Eco (=4) are mandatory
  • To edit a particular schedule, you need to enter the schedule_id, if you don't specify one, the modification will be applied to the current schedule


Scope to use:
write_thermostat

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: schedule_id
parameters.description: Id of the schedule. If not specified, it modifies the active schedule.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the schedule

parameters.name: zones
parameters.description: Array of zones. Zones describe the heating behavior in each room. More info on the format of a zone here.

parameters.name: timetable
parameters.description: Array of timetables. Timetables apply a zone for a specific duration. More info on the format of a timetable here.

parameters.name: hg_temp
parameters.description: Frost guard temperature value

parameters.name: away_temp
parameters.description: Away temperature value

post.description:
This endpoint permits to apply a specific schedule to a home

Scope to use:
write_thermostat

parameters.name: home_id
parameters.description: Home ID

parameters.name: schedule_id
parameters.description: Id of the schedule to switch on

reachable.description: false if none of the module of the room are reachable

heating_power_request.description: Represents the percentage of heating the valve of a room are asking for

therm_setpoint_mode.description: mode (can be manual, max, off, schedule, away or hg)

therm_setpoint_start_time.description: start time for a manual setpoint

therm_setpoint_end_time.description: end time for a manual setpoint

boiler_control.description: how the product is able to communicate with the boiler

boiler_valve_comfort_boost.description: whether or not a valve is asking for a boost

boiler_status.description: whether the boiler is ON or OFF

anticipating.description: whether a device is currently anticipating or not

boiler_valve_comfort_boost.description: whether or not a valve is asking for a boost

boiler_status.description: whether the boiler is ON or OFF

reachable.description: true if the module can communicate with the relay

therm_mode.description: mode of the home (schedule, away, frost_guard)

room_id.description: room where the module is placed
room where the module is placed

module_ids.description: array of the modules associated to this room

type.description: type of the zone (0 = day, 1 = night, 2 = away, 3 = frost guard, 4 = custom, 5 = eco, 8 = comfort)

SyncError.description: Error from physical devices such as the device can't parse the parameter

id.description: device/module id that causes the error

code.description:
error code
* 1 - Unknown error
* 2 - Internal error
* 3 - Parser error
* 4 - Command unknown node module error
* 5 - Command invalid params
* 6 - Unreachable
